claims.evidence_text: By setting the first derivative dy/dx=0 revealed that N fertilizer rate to achieve maximum grain yield of wheat was 207 kg ha −1.
claims.claim_summary: N fertilizer rate to achieve maximum grain yield of wheat in calcareous soils of West Delta, Egypt was 207 kg N/ha.

claims.evidence_text: The general recommendation for N fertilizer for wheat grown on calcareous soils of Egypt is approximately 240 kg ha −1, although studies on wheat in the same region have suggested that lower N fertilizer rates can be used without compromising grain yield.
claims.claim_summary: The general N fertilizer recommendation for wheat on calcareous soils of Egypt is about 240 kg N/ha.

claims.evidence_text: six N application rates of 0, 50, 100, 150, 200, and 250 kg N ha −1 in four equal split doses as ammonium nitrate at 0, 30, 45, and 60 days after sowing (DAS).
claims.claim_summary: N fertilizer was applied in four equal split doses at 0, 30, 45, and 60 days after sowing; wheat response to N after Feekes 6 (around 55 DAS) is low.

claims.evidence_text: A critical N dilution curve was developed as: Nc=50.141 W^-0.424 ... where Nc is total N concentration in shoot (g kg −1 DM), W is total shoot biomass (Mg DM ha −1).
claims.claim_summary: Critical shoot N dilution curve for wheat in calcareous soils: Nc = 50.141 * W^-0.424 (Nc in g N/kg shoot DM, W in Mg DM/ha).

claims.evidence_text: Depending on the rate of N application and growth stage, N concentrations ranged from 41.0 to 15.7 g kg −1 DM.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat shoot N concentration declined over the growing season from 41.0 to 15.7 g N/kg DM depending on N rate and growth stage.

claims.evidence_text: The global N use efficiency of cereal crops has remained alarmingly low at around 42% (Zhang et al. 2015).
claims.claim_summary: Global N use efficiency of cereal crops is around 42%.

claims.evidence_text: Giza 171 wheat cultivar was manually sowed in early November at a sowing rate of 155 kg ha −1 and harvested in mid-April.
claims.claim_summary: Spring wheat (cv. Giza 171) was sown at a seeding rate of 155 kg/ha in Egypt.

claims.evidence_text: Giza 171 wheat cultivar was manually sowed in early November ... and harvested in mid-April.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat in West Delta, Egypt was sown in early November and harvested in mid-April.

claims.evidence_text: Grain and straw yields were recorded at 12% moisture content and on dry weight basis, respectively.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat grain yield was recorded at 12% moisture content.
